mechanism_narrative: A downward move in FEDFUNDS over the prior month lowers the discount rate and signals easier financial conditions, which lifts equity multiples and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or below baseline in more than 55% of months with a falling FEDFUNDS rate, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises at least 0.3 percentage points over three months, labor-market deterioration raises recession risk and compresses earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates broad consumption and payroll expansion, which signals stronger near-term revenue growth and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition RSAFS up and PAYEMS up does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered months fail to deliver positive excess 20-day SPX returns relative to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in INDPRO over the prior month reflects stronger industrial supply output and improved cyclical momentum, which tends to lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprice growth sensitivity.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly changes in INDPRO are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than negative changes, or if the short-horizon sign relation vanishes in rolling 1-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST below its recent trend signals weakening housing demand and construction activity, which foreshadows broader cyclical softening and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below trend is not followed by below-average 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the effect is positive in a majority of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains very low, real rates are likely more negative and liquidity conditions are supportive, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises while FEDFUNDS < 1%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-rate and rising-inflation condition does not produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears once either proxy is conditioned on separately.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market stress regime, rising PAYEMS after a prior decline signals stabilization in employment and reduces downside earnings risk, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS turns up after a 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS rebounds fail to outperform in months where UNRATE is elevated, or if 60-day SPX returns are not positive after the trigger in a majority of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A positive GDPC1 print after a weak prior quarter signals renewed real demand growth, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns positive after a prior contraction
failure_condition: Reject if GDP reacceleration episodes do not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the average forward return is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
